insights.title: Brain operating doctrine — show the WHOLE map
insights.content: Operating principle for brain.blackseaspv.com (and for me when working with Adrián): never reduce him to a checklist. He explicitly stated: 'ja funugjem na vizualzácii veľkých cielov a plánov... vždy som sníval a chcel a chcem aj dnes... aj ked moja hlava má v sebe často veľké temno, smuto a černotu'. PRACTICAL IMPLICATIONS: (1) Map view (§01) is PRIMARY — it shows the whole multi-business system at once; (2) Today view (§00) is for dopamine ticks but NEVER becomes the default — that would collapse the picture; (3) When proposing new work, frame it within the 3-business architecture first, THEN drill to tactical tasks; (4) Insights view (§06) is where the THINKING lives — must keep growing; (5) Inbox (§07) catches half-thoughts so they don't get lost in the dark periods; (6) Sticky notes on map = floating thoughts on Adrián's mental canvas, NEVER auto-organize them away.

insights.title: Comfort with complex environment clients — operational stance
insights.content: Direct quote: 'už som jednal s rôznymi ľuďmi a nemám s tým problém'. Adrián has dealt with non-standard clients before through A27 + Black Sea SPV OTC work. This is not naïveté but earned position — he knows where the compliance lines are because he's the one who built them. Implication for client triage: 'complex' is fine as a client attribute IF deal hits ≥2 of military/finance/compliance AND clears red lines (no sanctioned persons / shell opacity / UBO nominee evasion / sanctions evasion facilitation / dummy wallets). Adrián is NOT looking for 'easy' clients — he's looking for clients where his expertise is the differentiating value. A 'difficult' client at Bitmama or PashaPay scale is exactly the kind of work that justifies €25-75K engagements.

insights.title: Public procurement & 'War Dogs' archetype — nuanced read
insights.content: Adrián's actual position (not War Dogs as cautionary tale): public procurement — Slovak VO, US gov contracts, Dubai — represents a genuine pattern where one tender has zero applicants because nobody understands compliance + dual-use + military overlap. He's not naive about War Dogs ('to bol prúser') — uses it as ARCHETYPE for the pattern of finding complex scaled deals with few competitors. Translation for Black Sea: NOT actual arms procurement (compliance kills us). YES the pattern applied to: cross-border crypto compliance under emerging frameworks (Nigeria ARIP, AIFC), defense-fund advisory where most consultancies have no fund-launch credibility, AZ pre-licensing positioning. Same underlying instinct (find complex, scaled, low-competition deal flow) at radically lower criminal risk.

insights.title: Multi-role positioning across entities
insights.content: Active operational roles: Fund Manager A27 IC SICAV (Malta, separating), Managing Director A27 Management Services (Prague), Owner Black Sea SPV (Bratislava), CCO Opportunity Fund (Amsterdam), External CCO + Operator AIR Labs (BVI/US Seena). FIVE distinct hats across 4 jurisdictions. Each one is verifiable and clean. This is what credibility looks like for a defense fund advisory pitch — not a deck about 'experience' but a registry trail across MFSA/AFM/CFR/BVI. Use sparingly in outreach (no resume dump) but make available in data room. Implication: A27 separation logistics must close cleanly Q3 2026 — current dual MD+Fund Manager status is the messiest variable.

insights.title: Adrián's attraction zones (what energizes vs. what drains)
insights.content: Quote: 'laka ma aj svery military/finance/ complaice'. These are the three zones where Adrián naturally lights up. Defense Capital Advisory = military × finance × compliance — perfect overlap = bm_defense exists. Frontier Markets = finance × compliance × complex jurisdiction = bm_frontier exists. Black Sea SPV OTC = finance × crypto compliance = bm_otc. Implication for picking deals: when a prospect inquiry comes in, primary filter is 'does this hit at least 2 of military/finance/compliance?' If no — politely decline or refer out. If yes — accept and let purpose vs. depression dynamic kick in. This is the working filter, not 'will it pay X'.

insights.title: Anti-decision: NO separate US LLC for 'complex environment clients'
insights.content: ADHD-stress mode briefly proposed: 'alebo založil black sea niekde v usa anonymne a pod ešte' — separate anonymous US LLC for 'kokoti who pay big.' Considered, pushed back, rejected. Reasons: (1) anonymous US LLC for opacity = exactly the structure that gets you flagged in DD; (2) Adrián's enforcement profile (former MFSA fund manager + lawsuit visibility) makes him a high-quality target for prosecutors making examples; (3) volume of legitimate work in defense + frontier markets is enough — no need for opacity premium. Red lines locked from this discussion: no sanctioned persons, no shell opacity, no UBO nominee evasion, no facilitation of sanctions evasion. Captured in note_no_baddeals sticky note.

insights.title: 3-business architecture — final lock (v2)
insights.content: Adrián's original framing (from transcript): 'kludne to sprav tak že Black sea je brand mozog a z toho idu business modely čo sme tu rieišli. eu defeence, consulting, potom to čo je teraz na webe čo posobí ako dual use pre ruských kokotov ale napríklad aj to čo sme riešili teraz azerbajdžan atd.' Final mapping: bm_otc (existing dual-use OTC at blackseaspv.com + AIR Labs Seena), bm_defense (EU defense fund advisory, new), bm_frontier (AZ/KZ AIFC/Nigeria, new). Brand colors: Steel/Signal Green/Alert. No 4th business. ALL future Black Sea work falls in one of these three buckets — if it doesn't, it's a sticky note in Inbox, not a new business model.

insights.title: Tax exposure reality check (Slovakia)
insights.content: Adrián's self-assessment: 'možno nepodané daňové priznanie na Slovensku alebo niečo, ale moja expozícia je do 1000€ na týchto veciach.' = small ADHD-bordel typical, NOT a structural problem. Counterweight: years of clean track record — built structures, moved millions across the world, everything clean+upraced+documented. Profile of someone who knows where the lines are (compliance officer mindset). Implication: not a blocker for licensed work. But: any backlog (unfiled returns, late filings) should be cleaned up Q3 2026 BEFORE any Defense Capital Advisory family-office prospect runs DD on Adrián personally.

insights.title: Adrián track record — exits & build patterns
insights.content: Prior exits (verifiable): Komoio (IP sale), Ridelo (patent sale). Recent build: A27 IC SICAV — built from idea to Bloomberg ticker. This pattern is rare. Most crypto people can't finish a fund; most fund people can't move with startup speed. Adrián has documented both. Implication for positioning: NOT 'compliance consultant who knows crypto.' YES 'operator who built a regulated fund and exited IP and now offers what only operators can — full-stack structuring + execution.' This is what justifies €25-75K fund structuring price points and €5-15K/mo Fractional CCO retainers vs. consultancy €2-5K commodity work.

insights.title: Notion workspace separation — AIR Labs is separate
insights.content: AIR Labs work lives in Seena's Notion workspace (Teamspace Home → Command Center). NOT in Black Sea HQ workspace. Brain at brain.blackseaspv.com is Adrián's command interface — it tracks AIR Labs AS A CLIENT engagement but doesn't replace AIR Labs's internal infrastructure. AIR Labs has its own agent system (CEO/CTO/Librarian/Doctor/CoderData/CoderStorage/CCO/CFO) and Notion is single source of truth for AIR Labs operations. Brain just shows Adrián's view of the engagement from Black Sea side.

insights.title: AIR Labs revenue potential — Adrián's view
insights.content: Phase 1: 20% Licence Fee on net Trading Revenue. With initial USD 50k capital and 30-50% annualized returns target: Adrián's licence fee = €3-5k/year initially, scaling with AUM. Phase 2: shifts to fund manager economics (1.5% mgmt + 20% carry). At €500k AUM = €29.5k/yr to Adrián (post-split with Seena). At €2M AUM = €118k/yr. At €10M AUM = €590k/yr. This is the LONG TERM cashflow anchor for Black Sea SPV — recurring, predictable, scaling with track record.

insights.title: AIR Labs security architecture — non-negotiable
insights.content: Per agreement Part V — mandatory on all exchange accounts: Hardware 2FA (YubiKey ~€60), Authy backup, dedicated email with hardware 2FA, withdrawal whitelist Seena-only with 48h lock, Seena's API keys IP-whitelisted, anti-phishing codes, sub-accounts for all trading (main account admin only), Bitwarden/1Password unique per exchange, always-on VPN. Emergency protocol: Adrián detects threat → Signal/Telegram alert → Seena executes withdrawal via API → assets secured in 1-4 hours. SK legal process takes 6-18 months → Seena always has time.

insights.title: AIR Labs Phase 3 roadmap (Month 18-36)
insights.content: Target AUM €5M+ by Month 24, €5-10M by Month 36. Considerations: tokenization (Estonia digital share register / Liechtenstein TVTG / EU MiCA), Malta MFSA AIF authorization, institutional capital. Tokenization unlocks fractional ownership (€100 not €40k tickets), 24/7 secondary market, automated dividend via smart contracts. Cost: €22-50k for full tokenization legal + smart contract development. Total 36-month investment: €99k-213k. Total 36-month revenue potential: €500k-1M/year by Month 24-36.

insights.title: AIR Labs Phase 2 roadmap (Month 6-18)
insights.content: When Phase 1 track record (6+ months) is built and first interested LPs surface: form Czech s.r.o. (€3.3-6.4k Y1) + Czech QIIF investment fund (€40-70k legal + audit). QIIF allows max 20 investors, min CZK 1M per LP (~€40k), light ČNB regulation. Revenue model: 1.5% mgmt fee + 20% perf fee above 8% hurdle. At €500k AUM with 30% return: €29.5k/year. At €2M AUM: €118k/year. At €10M AUM: €590k/year. THIS is Adrián's long-term cashflow base.

insights.title: Phase 1 solution: Adrián personal accounts + dual-layer protection
insights.content: OPTION A from playbook chosen. Adrián opens personal exchange accounts on Binance/Bybit/Kraken/OKX as Slovak national. Seena gets full API + withdrawal rights protected by: (1) Withdrawal whitelist — Seena's wallets only, 24-48h exchange-enforced delay on changes; (2) Seena's own API keys with withdraw permission, IP-whitelisted; (3) Two legal agreements (Silent Partnership + Operational). Result: Adrián physically cannot redirect funds even if he wanted to. Composite risk to Seena's capital = VERY LOW. Time to operational: 2-5 days. Cost to Seena: €0.

insights.title: AIR Labs central problem: US Nexus
insights.content: Seena Houman is US national. Every regulated exchange (Binance, BingX, Bitget, KuCoin, Gate.io, Airwallex) auto-rejects AIR Labs BVI because of US beneficial owner. Driven by FinCEN BSA/AML + FATCA 30% withholding penalty rules. Cannot be fixed on BVI entity — structure itself is the problem. Solution: non-US person becomes UBO and director. That non-US person is Adrián (Slovak national, zero US nexus).

insights.title: Parallel cashflow vs. growth strategy
insights.content: Frontier Markets (Nigeria ARIP, AIFC gap) = FAST cashflow, lower per-deal, faster close (€8-60K, 2-12 weeks). Defense Capital Advisory = SLOW build, higher per-deal, longer close cycle (€25-75K + retainers, 60-180 days). Parallel execution: Frontier closes first deals in Q2-Q3 to fund overhead and counsel retainer; Defense Capital builds via Substack + warm network through Q3-Q4 for institutional retainers starting Q4/Q1 2027. Both feed back into brand authority on the other.

Three business models: (1) Black Sea SPV — operational base, OTC + crypto + AIR Labs work; (2) Defense Capital Advisory — growth bet, EU defense + dual-use fund advisory; (3) Frontier Markets — expansion, AZ + KZ AIFC + West Africa crypto. insights.content: Adrián referenced War Dogs in context of public procurement (Slovak + global VO). Acknowledged the film ended in disaster ('to bol prúser'). insights.title: ADHD multitrack mode — feature, not bug
insights.content: Direct quote: 'ano viem ej to matuce pre teba ko model ale proste tak funguje zajtra robíem zase na inej časti mojho business'. Don't try to linearize Adrián. He works on BM1 today, BM3 tomorrow, BM2 the day after — this is HIS pattern. Brain architecture (3 parallel business models with shared brand mozog) is designed for this. Same for assistant approach: don't force sequential focus, support all three in parallel.

insights.title: Purpose as antidepressant — Adrián's operating principle
insights.content: Direct quote: 'ked mám puspose niečo stavať a vízu depreis aide dosť do uzadia a môžem riešiť a som štastný a idealen aby to donieslo aj nejaké peniaze.' Building from 0 to working system is what gets Adrián out of the dark. Practical implication: brain UX must support BIG goals and BIG visualization (network graph, multi-business map) — not just micro tasks. Don't reduce him to a to-do list. The 'Today' view is the dopamine loop; the 'Map' view is the purpose anchor.

insights.content: Po diskusii o compliance: prijatý mindset B (compliance je často zle implementovaná ale pre tvoj profil treba reálnu compliance). Red lines nastavené v Frontier Markets Pack. Žiadne dummy wallets pre business income.

insights.title: Nigeria fastest cash window
insights.content: ISA 2025 + ARIP live. ₦500M minimum capital, penalty ₦10M/mes pre non-compliance. 50+ firiem v licensing wave. Toto je primárny target pre prvý cashflow.

insights.content: AFSA frameworks update 1.1.2026. 149 Authorised Firms + 30 DASPs musia adaptovať. 6-mes transition pre Crowdfunding (deadline jún-júl 2026). Sweet spot.

insights.content: Stablecoins legal April 2026. 3 licensed exchanges, 4 token issuers. NAPP regulator active and reachable. Zero EU advisory presence. Direct contacts available for all licensed players.

insights.content: New virtual assets law signed Jan 2026. $11.4B turnover in 7 months 2025. 13 licensed exchanges. IMF April 2026 recommended stronger AML/CFT monitoring. Black Sea Compliance Advisory is exactly that. Zero EU advisors present.

insights.title: Georgia NBG crackdown creates peak compliance demand
insights.content: NBG enforcing VASP registration since Oct 2025. Grey-market OTC desks and unregistered brokers must register or close. Registration + AML framework = direct Black Sea product, EUR 8-15K.

insights.content: OKX: direct Telegram institutional channel exists. Kraken: existing account manager relationship (Mats). These are the highest-probability big-ticket leads — warm intros beat cold email.

insights.title: Full Attack Mode launched - 13 markets, 10 channels
insights.content: May 27-28, 2026: Adrian ordered 'rozbehni vsetky kanaly okrem linkedin a rozheni vsetko co sa da'. Result: 85+ Gmail drafts, 10 BD channels designed, 3 meetings booked, 6 VC pitches, 5 regtech partnerships, 5 media pitches, full Twitter/Telegram/FATF playbooks. Markets expanded from original 3 (AZ/KZ/Nigeria) to 13+ (adding Turkey, Kuwait, Ghana, SA, Pakistan, Kenya, Kyrgyzstan, Georgia, UAE deep dive). Key constraint: domain reputation - max 10-15 emails/day, Adrian decides what sends.

insights.title: Turkey CMB - 33-day window for 76 CASPs
insights.content: 76 CASPs must get CMB authorization certificates by June 30, 2026. No indication of extension. Biggest gap: firms have KYC tech but not written AML policies and MASAK reporting procedures. Key targets: Bitexen (just got ADGM license May 21 - dual jurisdiction play), Bitci (multi-market). AVOID: ICRYPEX (founder arrested for drug trafficking + murder).

insights.title: Nigeria CBN - personal liability changes the game
insights.content: CBN circular BSD/DIR/PUB/LAB/019/002: INDIVIDUAL compliance officers face personal regulatory exposure, not just the institution. This is the sharpest hook in any outreach. June 10 deadline for automated AML roadmaps. ISA 2025: DAX capital doubled from N500M to N2B.

insights.title: VC portfolio access - highest leverage channel
insights.content: One meeting with a VC = access to 20-100 portfolio companies who ALL need compliance. CV VC alone = 77 African blockchain investments. Shorooq = 50+ MENA+Pakistan companies. Pitch: free 30-min diagnostics for portfolio founders. Zero cost to the VC. This is the highest-ROI channel if even one VC says yes.

insights.title: Domain reputation is sacred - never burn it
insights.content: Adrian's exact words: 'do not do that because you will burn the domain'. Max 10-15 emails per day. Always present drafts first. Adrian decides what goes out. Domain blackseaspv.com is fragile. One spam report could destroy deliverability. This constraint shapes everything about the outreach strategy.

insights.title: Kenya - the scramble is coming
insights.content: 50+ crypto firms operating in Kenya. Zero licensed. November 2026 hard deadline. VASP Act 2025 live. Draft regs published March 2026, comment period closed April 10. But licensing has NOT started because final regulations are pending. FATF grey-listed since Feb 2024. When licensing opens, it will be a stampede.

insights.title: Pakistan PVARA - greenest greenfield in crypto regulation
insights.content: Virtual Assets Act 2026. Brand new federal authority. 8 license categories. Sandbox guidelines launched April 2026. Currently prioritizing firms already licensed elsewhere (Binance and HTX have NOCs). As sandbox opens, new applicants need AML/CFT from day one. Zero EU advisory presence. First-mover advantage massive.

insights.title: FATF plenary = biggest BD moment 3x per year
insights.content: June 2026 plenary (final under Mexican Presidency). Countries to watch: Kenya, Kuwait, Nigeria, Turkey, Pakistan, SA. Window: be among the FIRST to publish analysis. Most firms take 24-48h. We publish within 2-4h. Pre-written templates ready for all scenarios. Multi-channel blast: Twitter, Substack, email, Telegram. This is the single highest-impact content event in the compliance calendar.

insights.title: Defense Capital Advisory BD attack launching
insights.content: May 28, 2026: Adrian realized we completely forgot Defense Capital Advisory in the BD blitz. This is the HIGHEST TICKET business model (€25-75K per engagement, €3.5-15K/mo retainers). EU defense spending explosion creates massive fund formation demand. Emerging GPs need someone who has actually built an MFSA-regulated fund from zero. That is Adrian. Launching: research defense VC targets, draft outreach, write Substack content, speaker applications.

insights.title: EU defense spending boom = fund formation boom
insights.content: EU Defense Roadmap March 2026 opens defense sector to innovation. Defense Equity Facility: €500M+ equity (EIF-backed). Proposed €1B Fund-of-Funds. NATO 5% GDP commitment. EDF €1B in 2026. This capital needs fund structures. Emerging GPs with defense-tech deal flow need compliant fund vehicles. Most law firms can do fund formation. Almost none have someone who built an MFSA SICAV AND understands crypto/dual-use compliance. Adrian is that person. The defense fund structuring market is underserved by people with actual operator experience.

insights.title: Defense and Frontier are not separate - they feed each other
insights.content: Defense fund managers need cross-border compliance (some invest in MENA, CIS, frontier markets). Frontier market crypto firms sometimes have defense/dual-use technology angles. The credibility from closing frontier market deals (real compliance work, real regulatory deadlines) strengthens the defense pitch ('we do this under pressure, in hard jurisdictions'). Vice versa: defense fund credentials (MFSA, MiFID II) strengthen the frontier pitch ('we are not just consultants, we built a regulated fund'). Both business models reinforce each other.

insights.title: Defense BD needs LinkedIn - unlike crypto, defense GPs live there
insights.content: For frontier markets (crypto), LinkedIn is not needed - Telegram, Twitter, email work. For defense capital advisory, LinkedIn IS the primary channel. Fund managers, family office CIOs, defense-tech founders - they are all on LinkedIn. When Adrian is ready to activate LinkedIn, defense content should be the primary use case. Prepare content now, activate when ready.

Key findings: TIER 1 targets (1st-time GPs, highest need): Darkstar Coalition (EST), D3 VC (UKR), ScaleWolf (LIT), Presto Tech Horizons (CZE - Prague!), Green Flag Ventures (USA/UKR). TIER 2 targets (scaling GPs): Expeditions Fund (POL, NATO-backed), Hyperion (ESP, EUR 500M Fund II), Join Capital (DEU, EIF's largest defense commitment), Karista (FRA, CNES), OTB Ventures (POL, NATO). Accelerator partnerships: NATO DIANA (150 companies), EUDIS (EU, EUR 1.5B budget). Investor networks: EDIN = highest priority for immediate membership. Events: SuperReturn Tech Sovereignty (Berlin Jun 11) = #1 priority. Czech target Presto is particularly interesting - Prague proximity for Adrian.

insights.detail: GLG, AlphaSights, Guidepoint, Third Bridge pay EUR 400-1200/hour for expert calls. Adrian's combination of MFSA/MiFID II + frontier markets + defense = rare expert profile. Onboarding: 1-2 weeks. Could generate EUR 2-8K/month immediately.

insights.title: EDF grant advisory = potential EUR 150-400K success fees per deal
insights.detail: EU defense grant procurement advisory uses success-based models. 3-8% success fee on grant amount. For EUR 5M EDF grant = EUR 150-400K in success fees. One firm reported 71% success rate securing EUR 190M+ in EDF funding. HIGH MARGIN adjacency.

insights.description: The MLRO was reclassified from Designated Individual to Approved Individual under amended AFSA General Rules (Jan 2026). insights.description: Independent DNS verification: SPF PASS, DKIM PASS, DMARC CONFIGURED (p=none). All 19 hard bounces on May 28 were wrong email addresses, not domain reputation issues. The coinuz.net 554.5.7.1 bounce was a forwarding chain failure at the receiving server.
